author: Philip Ball
title: The Trouble with Scientists
desc: Science is distorted by human bias; researchers favor confirmation while journals reward 'neat' results, leading to a slow self-correction process despite improved transparency tools.

author: Paul Graham
title: Cities and Ambition
desc: live where people chase X → you start chasing X; cities quietly set the default definition of success and pull you towards it.

author: PALLADIUM
title: The Past and Future of Military Drones
desc: how drones quietly shifted war from 'protect pilots' to 'scale cheap, autonomous kill systems'—and why the next leap is less about hardware and more about coordination and mass.

author: George Mack
title: High Agency in 30 Minutes
desc: The mindset that separates people who make things happen from people who wait for them to happen. Short, punchy, worth re-reading once a year.
